attacking_style: Primary sprint finisher who relies on positioned lead-outs to contest mass sprints. Not an attacker in the traditional sense—waits for organized bunch finishes.
team_tactics: Team delivers him to sprint finishes with organized positioning. Demonstrates consistent lead-out train execution across multiple race editions.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where organized bunch finishes are guaranteed. Avoids or struggles on selective terrain that fragments the peloton before finale.
vulnerability: Time gaps and positioning—losing races by single-second margins suggests weakness in time bonuses or decisive positioning. May struggle when not winning despite strong form.
key_patterns: Exceptional Asian circuit specialist: 12 stage wins across 8 race editions demonstrates regional dominance
Peak performance concentration: 7 wins in single early career edition shows explosive purple patch capability
Sprint finish consistency: Multiple stage victories confirm reliable sprint execution in bunch kicks
form_trajectory: positive: 12 career stage wins with ongoing sprint success, though losing by 1-second margins suggests minor tactical refinement needed
